title: Arabic Pronouns and Their Attached Forms: Huwa, Hiya, ka, and ya
summary: This session teaches detached and attached pronouns in Modern Standard Arabic, focusing on their usage to replace repeated nouns. The teacher explains how attached pronouns like ka (your) and ya (my) modify nouns, emphasizing examples and avoiding overcomplication with advanced grammar.
key_points: Arabic uses both detached (e.g., ه֑وا - huwa, ه֓ي - hiya) and attached pronouns.
كְ (ka/your) attaches to nouns for possession: e.g., عناك (ismuka = your name).
يְ (ya/my) modifies nouns: عنوا (کنی - yameen = my right hand).
Attached pronouns indicate possession without repeating the noun, similar to English 'my', 'your'.
